steps.canonical_text: Tell one person to call emergency services now and another to bring an AED if one is nearby. If you are alone with an adult, call on speaker and start compressions immediately.
Kneel beside the chest. Place the heel of one hand on the center of the chest on the lower half of the breastbone, then place your other hand on top and lock your elbows.

asset_refs.caption: Place the heel of one hand in the center of the chest
asset_refs.content_description: A rescuer placing both hands in the center of the chest for CPR compressions.

steps.canonical_text: Push hard and fast about 5 centimeters deep at a rate of 100 to 120 per minute. Count out loud and let the chest fully rise between pushes.
When the AED arrives, turn it on and follow the voice prompts. Expose the chest, attach the pads, and keep doing compressions whenever the AED is not analyzing or shocking.

asset_refs.caption: Turn the AED on and follow the voice prompts
asset_refs.content_description: A gloved hand pressing the power button on an AED.

asset_refs.caption: Attach the AED pads to the exposed chest as shown
asset_refs.content_description: An AED pad being attached to an adult chest in the recommended position.

asset_refs.caption: For a child, attach the AED pads in the position shown on the pad diagrams
asset_refs.content_description: An AED pad being attached to a child chest using the placement shown on the pad.

steps.canonical_text: Do not stop compressions except for AED analysis or shock. If another helper is available, switch every two minutes to avoid fatigue. Continue until emergency services take over or the person starts breathing normally.
